glossary.definition: The two-detector correlation function E(a,b) for a Bell-type experiment. For the singlet state it equals minus the analyzer dot product.
The scalar combination S = E(a,b) + E(a,b') + E(a',b) - E(a',b') used in the Clauser-Horne-Shimony-Holt inequality.
A pure unit quaternion u_a in the imaginary subspace of H used to encode an analyzer direction a in the quaternionic spin geometry of this paper.
The real component of a quaternion. In this paper the scalar part of u_a u_b reproduces the Bell correlation law.
The antisymmetric two-qubit state |Psi^-> = (|01> - |10>)/sqrt(2), equivalently the rotationally invariant spin-0 state used in Bell and CHSH derivations.
The quantum-mechanical upper bound |S| <= 2 sqrt(2) for the CHSH value.
The geometric content of the CHSH derivation arising from noncommuting local observables. In this paper it is written as a cross-product statement in quaternionic language.
